rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the implementation scope when binding to implementation-level ports or connections.
Ensure all AGREE variables refer to explicitly declared AADL features or constants within the target scope.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture integrity by only referencing visible features in the annex target scope.
Attach contracts to the most specific scope that owns the referenced features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Use equations or guarantees only for variables bound to ports or constants in the current scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Avoid placing component-type-level contracts in implementation scopes without corresponding feature bindings.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
